title: Day 44: El País Confirms O3 as New CJNG Leader; Succession Vacuum Enters Seventh Week
detail: Day 44 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Spanish newspaper El País published a detailed confirmation on April 6 identifying Juan Carlos Valencia González ('O3') as CJNG's new leader — the most credible international media confirmation of the succession to date. O3 (born September 12, 1984 in Santa Ana, California) is El Mencho's stepson and commander of Grupo Élite, CJNG's paramilitary enforcement wing. His U.S. citizenship complicates targeting options and creates unique bilateral legal tensions. The succession field has been significantly narrowed: El Doble R (Ricardo Ruiz Velasco) arrested March 11; El Tuli (Hugo César Macías Ureña) killed February 22; El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary unresolved rival with a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty and regional power bases in Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, Michoacán, and Guerrero. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 67+ officials detained across Jalisco, Michoacán, Morelos, and additional states. Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 continues through April 12. U.S.-Mexico Joint Intelligence Targeting Center (JITC-CC) remains at high operational tempo.
